findings.description: Repeated header/footer content inserted between page-equivalent sections disrupts reading order
findings.evidence: In HTML chunk 1, the official names (PAT McCRORY Governor, DONALD R. VAN DER VAART Secretary, TRACY DAVIS Director) appear twice: once in the letterhead area at top and again as plain text below the colored bar before the MEMORANDUM heading. Footer text ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality...') and a colored bar are also inserted between the letterhead and memo content. In the PDF page 1, the names appear only once in the letterhead.

findings.description: Footer and decorative bar inserted between content sections disrupts reading flow
findings.evidence: In HTML chunk 2, footer text ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality...') and a colored bar appear between the outline (sections I-IV) and the 'I. History/Background' heading. In PDF page 2, these sections flow directly without intervening footer/header material.

findings.description: A repeated PDF page-header image from page 2 is inserted mid-content in the HTML body (inside a <section>, just before the 'I. History/Background' heading), rather than being removed or consolidated into the single <header>.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains:

I. History/Background

... This is a duplicate of the NC DEQ page header already rendered in the
element above. The PDF repeats this header image on every page, but the HTML should consolidate it once.
